title: ggen Security Definition of Done
description: Comprehensive specification of 8 cryptographic security dimensions for ggen v26.5.28. Every security-critical feature must validate against ALL 8 dimensions before claiming completion.

security_dimensions.name: Receipt Generation
security_dimensions.short_description: Every sync emits .ggen/receipts/latest.json with cryptographic signature
security_dimensions.what_it_is: Receipt generation is the process of creating a cryptographically signed JSON proof that a ggen sync operation completed with specific inputs and outputs. Each receipt captures the operation's identity, timestamp, input/output hashes, and an Ed25519 digital signature. Receipts are the primary mechanism for proving what happened during a generation run.

key_types: Receipt { operation_id: String, timestamp: DateTime<Utc>, input_hashes: Vec<String>, output_hashes: Vec<String>, signature: String, previous_receipt_hash: Option<String> }
file_locations: .ggen/receipts/latest.json — symlink or copy pointing to most recent receipt

validation_command.description: Print human-readable fields from a receipt file without signature verification

failure_modes.mode: Decorative completion — receipt not written
failure_modes.symptom: ggen sync exits 0 but .ggen/receipts/latest.json does not exist or is unchanged
failure_modes.root_cause: emit_receipt() was skipped or failed silently
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — no proof of what ran

failure_modes.mode: Contract drift — signature field empty
failure_modes.symptom: ggen receipt info shows signature_present: false OR ggen receipt verify returns is_valid: false
failure_modes.root_cause: Receipt::sign() was never called or signing key was not loaded
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — receipt is unsigned, unverifiable

failure_modes.mode: Epistemic bypass — hardcoded operation_id
failure_modes.symptom: All receipts have the same operation_id (e.g., 'dummy-op' or UUID all zeros)
failure_modes.root_cause: operation_id is hardcoded instead of generated per-run
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — cannot distinguish between different runs

failure_modes.mode: Fail-open — non-empty hashes not enforced
failure_modes.symptom: ggen receipt info shows input_hashes: 0 and output_hashes: 0
failure_modes.root_cause: Hashes are optional or default to empty Vec; should be populated from pipeline
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — receipt does not capture what was generated

recovery: If receipt is missing: Re-run ggen sync with --audit true to force receipt emission
If signature is empty: Regenerate keys with 'ggen init --force-keys' and re-run sync
If hashes are empty: Verify pipeline is populating input_hashes and output_hashes before emit_receipt()
If operation_id is hardcoded: Ensure Uuid::new_v4() is called, not a constant string

sabotage_tests.required_outcome: Exit 0; new .ggen/receipts/latest.json created with valid signature and non-empty hashes
Exit 0; new keypair generated, receipt signed with new key
ggen receipt verify returns is_valid: false (tampering detected)

security_dimensions.name: Signature Validation (Ed25519)
security_dimensions.what_it_is: Every receipt is signed with an Ed25519 private key (signing.key) and can be verified with the corresponding public key (verifying.key). Ed25519 provides cryptographic proof that a receipt has not been tampered with. Signature validation is mandatory before accepting any receipt as proof.

key_types: Receipt { signature: String (hex-encoded Ed25519 signature bytes) }
file_locations: .ggen/keys/signing.key — private key, hex-encoded, MUST NOT be committed to version control
.ggen/keys/verifying.key — public key, hex-encoded, SAFE to commit (or store separately)
.ggen/keys/public.pem — alternative name for verifying key (checked by ggen receipt verify)

validation_command.description: Verify Ed25519 signature on a receipt using the public key

failure_modes.mode: Bypass — signature not verified
failure_modes.symptom: ggen receipt verify always returns is_valid: true without actually calling Ed25519::verify()
failure_modes.root_cause: Verification step was skipped or mocked
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — tampering is never detected

failure_modes.mode: Contract drift — signature computed on wrong message
failure_modes.symptom: Valid receipt becomes invalid after innocent field change; or tampered receipt still verifies
failure_modes.detection: Edit receipt JSON to change timestamp, re-run verify — should fail
failure_modes.root_cause: Receipt::signing_message() does not canonicalize or uses wrong fields
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — signature does not cover all data

failure_modes.mode: Key management failure — private key exposed
failure_modes.symptom: .ggen/keys/signing.key appears in git history or is world-readable
failure_modes.root_cause: Private key was written to repo or file permissions not enforced
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — attacker can forge receipts

failure_modes.mode: Fail-open — missing public key is not an error
failure_modes.symptom: ggen receipt verify returns is_valid: true when .ggen/keys/verifying.key does not exist
failure_modes.detection: rm .ggen/keys/verifying.key; ggen receipt verify .ggen/receipts/latest.json — should fail
failure_modes.root_cause: do_verify() has a fallback that returns is_valid: true on missing key
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — verification can be bypassed by deleting the key

failure_modes.mode: Legacy path contamination — old unsigned receipts still accepted
failure_modes.symptom: Receipts with empty signature field still return is_valid: true
failure_modes.root_cause: do_verify() checks signature_present but still returns true
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — unsigned receipts are accepted

recovery: If key is compromised: Delete .ggen/keys/signing.key and regenerate with ggen init --force-keys
If signature verification fails: Check that verifying.key matches the private key that signed the receipt
If key file is missing: Regenerate with ggen init; all future receipts will use the new key
If verification returns false for a legitimate receipt: Check file permissions and key format (must be hex-encoded)

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Tamper with receipt JSON after signing
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: is_valid: false (tampering detected by signature mismatch)
is_valid: false (invalid signature bytes)
is_valid: false; error message says 'Public key required'

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Use wrong public key to verify
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: is_valid: false (signature from different key pair)

security_dimensions.name: Lockfile Digests
security_dimensions.short_description: Every pack entry in .ggen/packs.lock has SHA-256 digest, verified at sync time
security_dimensions.what_it_is: The lockfile (.ggen/packs.lock) records the set of packages installed and the exact hash (SHA-256) of each pack's TOML at install time. During ggen sync --locked, these digests are re-verified to detect if a pack has been modified or deleted. Digest verification ensures that the code generation input has not changed unexpectedly.

key_types: Lockfile { version: u32, manifest_id: Uuid, packages: IndexMap<PackageId, PackageVersion>, created_at: DateTime<Utc> }
InstallationManifest { packages: Vec<(PackageId, PackageVersion, String)> } — third field is digest
key_functions: PackageInstaller::verify_integrity() — re-verify digests at sync time
file_locations: .ggen/packs.lock — lockfile with all packages and their digests

validation_command.description: Validate pack integrity by re-computing and comparing digests against .ggen/packs.lock

expected_output_invalid.status: Digest mismatch for acme/base; digest mismatch for acme/utils

structure.packages: IndexMap<PackageId, PackageVersion> — one entry per installed pack

enforcement: digest must be computed from actual pack TOML at install time, never default to empty string
digest must be re-verified before allowing sync --locked to proceed
A missing package or digest mismatch must hard-fail sync --locked with exit code 1

failure_modes.mode: Decorative completion — digest not computed
failure_modes.symptom: .ggen/packs.lock written but digest field is empty or not present
failure_modes.root_cause: install.rs never called hash_data() on the pack TOML
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — tampering is not detected

failure_modes.mode: Fail-open — digest mismatch is logged but not enforced
failure_modes.symptom: ggen sync --locked logs 'digest mismatch for acme/base' but continues execution
failure_modes.detection: ggen sync --locked; grep 'digest mismatch' output; check exit code (must be non-zero)
failure_modes.root_cause: verify_integrity() returns a warning instead of Err
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — sync proceeds with tampered inputs

failure_modes.mode: Legacy path contamination — old lockfile format still supported
failure_modes.symptom: .ggen/packs.lock without digest field is still accepted
failure_modes.detection: Create lockfile missing digest field; run ggen sync --locked
failure_modes.root_cause: Parsing has a fallback that defaults digest to empty string
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — old (unverified) lockfiles are still trusted

failure_modes.mode: Contract drift — timestamp not recorded
failure_modes.symptom: .ggen/packs.lock lacks installed_at or installed_at is zero value
failure_modes.root_cause: Lockfile writer does not capture Utc::now() at install time
failure_modes.impact: MEDIUM — cannot distinguish between installations from different times

recovery: If digest is missing: Delete .ggen/packs.lock and re-run ggen packs add for each package
If digest mismatches: Check if pack TOML was modified (intended or not); if intended, reinstall pack; if unintended, replace pack with known-good version
If package is missing: Add it with ggen packs add; re-run ggen packs verify
If lockfile is corrupted: Delete and regenerate by re-running all ggen packs add commands in order

sabotage_tests.required_outcome: Exit 1; error message: 'Digest mismatch for acme/base'
Exit 1; error message: 'Package acme/base missing'
Exit 1; error message references digest mismatch

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Create empty digest field in lockfile
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: Exit 1; error message references empty or invalid digest

security_dimensions.name: Input/Output Hashing (BLAKE3)
security_dimensions.short_description: Receipts record all input hashes and output hashes (BLAKE3 or SHA-256)
security_dimensions.what_it_is: Every receipt captures BLAKE3 hashes of all inputs consumed during the pipeline (RDF ontology, packs, configs) and all outputs generated (source files, artifacts). These hashes form a complete record of what was input and what was produced, enabling reproducibility and tamper detection across the entire pipeline.

key_types: Each hash is a hex-encoded digest
key_functions: Pipeline stages (μ₁–μ₅) compute input/output hashes and pass to receipt

validation_command.cli: ggen receipt info <receipt-path> (shows counts) or inspect .ggen/receipts/latest.json
validation_command.description: Verify that input_hashes and output_hashes are populated and non-empty

reproduction_validation.procedure: Re-run ggen sync with identical inputs and compare output_hashes

invariants: Every input file consumed must appear in input_hashes with a non-empty SHA-256
Every output file written must appear in output_hashes with a non-empty SHA-256
Hash values must be 64-char hex strings (SHA-256) or 64-char hex (BLAKE3)
No hash value may be all zeros (0x0000...0000)
Hash ordering does not matter but duplicates are not allowed

failure_modes.mode: Fail-open — empty input_hashes
failure_modes.symptom: Receipt has input_hashes: [] (empty list)
failure_modes.root_cause: Pipeline did not compute input hashes or emit_receipt() does not populate them
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — no record of what inputs were used

failure_modes.mode: Fail-open — empty output_hashes
failure_modes.symptom: Receipt has output_hashes: [] (empty list)
failure_modes.root_cause: Pipeline did not emit output hashes or render stage failed silently
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — no record of what was generated

failure_modes.mode: Contract drift — hashes are defaults (all zeros)
failure_modes.symptom: input_hashes or output_hashes contain all-zero SHA-256 values
failure_modes.root_cause: Hashes computed from empty data or not computed at all
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — hashes do not reflect actual inputs/outputs

failure_modes.mode: Legacy path contamination — old format with dict instead of vec
failure_modes.symptom: input_hashes is an object { key: hash } instead of array [hash, hash, ...]
failure_modes.root_cause: Old receipt format is still accepted; parser has fallback
failure_modes.impact: MEDIUM — inconsistent hash format makes validation harder

recovery: If input_hashes is empty: Verify that all pipeline stages (μ₁–μ₅) are computing and logging input hashes
If output_hashes is empty: Verify that the render stage writes files and computes hashes before emitting receipt
If hashes are zeros: Check that hash computation is not being called on empty data; verify file paths are correct
If format inconsistent: Migrate old receipt format to new array format or re-run sync

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Delete input files before sync but after parsing
sabotage_tests.command: ggen sync (capture input_hashes); delete one input file; verify hash is still in receipt
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: input_hashes captured at parse time, not affected by later deletion

sabotage_tests.command: ggen sync; capture output_hashes; edit generated file; re-hash; compare
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: output_hashes no longer match (tampering detected)

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Omit a generated file from output_hashes
sabotage_tests.command: ggen sync; manually delete entry from receipt.output_hashes; verify signature fails
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: ggen receipt verify returns is_valid: false (signature covers all hashes)

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Inject an extra file into output_hashes
sabotage_tests.command: ggen sync; manually add non-existent file to receipt.output_hashes; verify signature fails
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: ggen receipt verify returns is_valid: false

security_dimensions.name: Chain Validation
security_dimensions.short_description: Receipt previous_hash links to prior receipt, unbroken chain from genesis
security_dimensions.what_it_is: Receipts form a cryptographic chain: each receipt (except the genesis receipt) contains the SHA-256 hash of the previous receipt in the field previous_receipt_hash. This chain provides a tamper-evident history of all sync operations, with each receipt cryptographically linked to its predecessor. Validating the chain ensures no receipts have been inserted, deleted, or reordered.

key_modules: ggen-cli/src/cmds/sync.rs::emit_receipt() — chains new receipt to latest
file_locations: .ggen/receipts/latest.json — points to most recent receipt in the chain

validation_command.cli: ggen receipt verify <receipt-path> (checks if previous_hash exists) or build ReceiptChain and call verify()
validation_command.description: Load all receipts from .ggen/receipts/ and verify the entire chain is unbroken
validation_command.example: for f in .ggen/receipts/sync-*.json; do jq '.previous_receipt_hash' $f; done | sort -u
validation_command.expected_output: Each receipt's previous_receipt_hash matches the hash of the chronologically prior receipt

chain_validation_procedure.step1: Load genesis receipt (first receipt, previous_receipt_hash: null)
chain_validation_procedure.step2: For each subsequent receipt, verify previous_receipt_hash == hash(prior_receipt)
chain_validation_procedure.step3: Verify all signatures in the chain
chain_validation_procedure.step4: Return Ok if all links are valid, Err if any link is broken

non_genesis_receipt.constraint_enforcement: ReceiptChain::append() enforces this; mismatch returns Err(HashMismatch)

chain_properties: Chain is ordered chronologically by receipt creation
No receipt can appear twice in the chain
No gaps in the chain (no deleted receipts)
All previous_receipt_hash values must be SHA-256 hashes (64-char hex)
Hashes must form a valid linked list: R₁ → R₂ → R₃ ... Rₙ

failure_modes.mode: Fail-open — broken chain is not detected
failure_modes.symptom: ReceiptChain::verify() returns Ok even when a link is broken
failure_modes.detection: Manually edit a receipt's previous_receipt_hash to a wrong value; call verify()
failure_modes.root_cause: verify() was not implemented or does not check previous_receipt_hash links
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — chain integrity is not validated

failure_modes.mode: Contract drift — previous_receipt_hash not populated
failure_modes.symptom: Receipt 2 has previous_receipt_hash: null even though it's not genesis
failure_modes.root_cause: Receipt::chain() was never called before signing
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — receipts are orphaned, no chain formed

failure_modes.mode: Legacy path contamination — old receipt format without chaining
failure_modes.symptom: Old receipts lack previous_receipt_hash field entirely; new chain breaks
failure_modes.detection: Create receipt without previous_receipt_hash field; try to append next receipt
failure_modes.root_cause: Parser has optional previous_receipt_hash; should reject missing field for non-genesis
failure_modes.impact: MEDIUM — old receipts cannot be chained with new ones

failure_modes.mode: Epistemic bypass — hard-coded previous_receipt_hash
failure_modes.symptom: All receipts have the same previous_receipt_hash value (not linked)
failure_modes.root_cause: previous_receipt_hash is hardcoded instead of computed from prior receipt
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — fake chain, not linked to prior operations

recovery: If chain is broken: Identify the corrupted receipt and delete it; later receipts may need re-emission
If previous_receipt_hash is missing: Delete all receipts after the first and re-run ggen sync
If chain is disconnected: Start a new chain from current state; archive old chain
If receipt is orphaned: Verify it was actually emitted (check filesystem mtime); if stale, delete

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Delete a receipt in the middle of the chain
sabotage_tests.command: ggen sync (creates R1); ggen sync (creates R2); rm .ggen/receipts/sync-*-first.json; ggen sync (creates R3)
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: R3.previous_receipt_hash does not match any existing receipt; chain validation fails
Chain validation should fail; ggen receipt verify returns is_valid: false due to signature mismatch

sabotage_tests.command: Manually swap timestamps/hashes in two receipts; attempt to load ReceiptChain
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: ReceiptChain::append() detects previous_receipt_hash mismatch; returns Err(HashMismatch)

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Create a gap in the chain (orphaned receipt)
sabotage_tests.command: Delete latest.json, creating a gap; ggen sync creates new receipt with wrong parent hash
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: New receipt is rejected or forms a separate chain; original chain is inaccessible

security_dimensions.name: Tampering Detection
security_dimensions.short_description: Signature verification fails if receipt or lockfile is modified after creation
security_dimensions.what_it_is: Any modification to a receipt's content (timestamp, hashes, operation_id, etc.) or to a lockfile (digest, version, packages) will cause signature or hash verification to fail. This is the core mechanism that prevents silent data corruption. Tampering can be detected by re-verifying the cryptographic signatures and comparing digests.

key_detection_mechanisms: Receipt signature verification: Ed25519 signature covers the exact receipt JSON; any change breaks signature
Lockfile digest verification: SHA-256 of pack TOML must match digest in lockfile
Hash chain validation: previous_receipt_hash must match the computed hash of the prior receipt
file_locations: Receipt: .ggen/receipts/*.json — tampered if signature verification fails
Lockfile: .ggen/packs.lock — tampered if any package digest is invalid

validation_command.cli: ggen receipt verify <receipt-path> && ggen packs verify
validation_command.description: Verify both receipt signatures and lockfile digests in one command
validation_command.example: ggen receipt verify .ggen/receipts/latest.json && ggen packs verify
validation_command.expected_output: Both commands return exit 0 and all_valid: true

tampering_scenarios.mechanism: Receipt::signing_message() includes all fields except signature; changing timestamp breaks signature
Signature covers input_hashes array; modification breaks signature
Signature covers output_hashes array; modification breaks signature
PackageInstaller::verify_integrity() recomputes digest and compares with lockfile entry
PackageInstaller attempts to resolve the modified version and fails

failure_modes.mode: Bypass — tampering is not detected
failure_modes.symptom: Modify a receipt and ggen receipt verify still returns is_valid: true
failure_modes.detection: Edit receipt JSON; run ggen receipt verify
failure_modes.root_cause: Receipt::verify() was not called, or signature verification was bypassed
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — tampering is invisible

failure_modes.mode: Fail-open — tampering is detected but execution continues
failure_modes.symptom: ggen sync --locked detects digest mismatch but continues with exit 0
failure_modes.detection: Modify lockfile digest; run ggen sync --locked; check exit code
failure_modes.root_cause: verify_integrity() returns a warning instead of Err; pipeline does not enforce it
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — corrupted data is silently used

failure_modes.mode: Legacy path — old non-signed receipts are accepted
failure_modes.symptom: Modify an old receipt (without signature field) and it is still accepted
failure_modes.detection: Create receipt with signature: ''; modify it; ggen receipt verify accepts it
failure_modes.root_cause: Verification was not enforced for old receipt format
failure_modes.impact: MEDIUM — unsigned receipts bypass verification

recovery: If receipt is tampered: Delete and re-run ggen sync to regenerate
If lockfile is tampered: Delete and reinstall packs with ggen packs add
If both are tampered: Start fresh with ggen init and reinstall everything
If unsure if tampering occurred: Compare files with version control history or backups

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Modify receipt in 5 different ways and verify tampering is detected
sabotage_tests.expected: All 5 modifications detected as tampering (is_valid: false)

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Modify lockfile digests and verify detection
modifications: Change one package digest to all zeros
Add an extra character to a digest
sabotage_tests.expected: All 5 modifications detected (ggen packs verify returns non-zero)

security_dimensions.name: Key Management
security_dimensions.short_description: Private keys never in repo, public keys verified, key rotation supported
security_dimensions.what_it_is: The signing key (private) and verifying key (public) are the cryptographic foundation of the entire security system. The private key must be kept secret and never committed to version control. The public key can be shared and verified. Key rotation must be supported so that compromised keys can be replaced without invalidating old receipts.

key_modules: ggen-cli/src/cmds/sync.rs::emit_receipt() — loads signing key, generates new keypair if missing
file_locations: .ggen/keys/signing.key — PRIVATE, hex-encoded ed25519 private key, MUST NOT be in git
.ggen/keys/verifying.key — PUBLIC, hex-encoded ed25519 public key, SAFE to commit

validation_command.description: Display the public key without exposing the private key

key_invariants.requirement: File mode must be 0600 (readable/writable by owner only)
key_invariants.enforcement: ggen init enforces chmod 0600 when writing signing.key
key_invariants.failure: File mode is 0644 or world-readable → CRITICAL EXPOSURE

key_invariants.invariant: Private key never committed to git
key_invariants.enforcement: ggen init creates .gitignore entry; git pre-commit hook checks
key_invariants.failure: signing.key appears in git history → CRITICAL COMPROMISE

key_invariants.invariant: Public key is hex-encoded and 64 chars
key_invariants.requirement: verifying.key is exactly 64 hex characters (32 bytes × 2)
key_invariants.failure: verifying.key is not exactly 64 chars → malformed key

key_invariants.invariant: Private key is hex-encoded and 64 chars
key_invariants.requirement: signing.key is exactly 64 hex characters (32 bytes × 2)
key_invariants.failure: signing.key is not exactly 64 chars → malformed key

key_invariants.requirement: verifying.key must be the public key corresponding to signing.key
key_invariants.enforcement: SigningKey::verifying_key() always derives the correct public key
key_invariants.failure: Mismatched key pair → verification will fail

failure_modes.mode: Key exposure — private key is world-readable
failure_modes.symptom: stat -c '%A' .ggen/keys/signing.key returns -rw-rw-rw-
failure_modes.detection: File permissions audit; git diff --check for exposed keys
failure_modes.root_cause: ggen init did not enforce 0600 permissions
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — any user can read private key

failure_modes.mode: Key exposure — private key committed to git
failure_modes.symptom: git log -p -- '.ggen/keys/signing.key' shows contents
failure_modes.root_cause: .gitignore does not include .ggen/keys/signing.key or .gitignore was not created
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — key is in version history forever

failure_modes.mode: Key mismatch — verifying.key does not match signing.key
failure_modes.symptom: ggen receipt verify fails even with a freshly signed receipt
failure_modes.detection: Generate new keypair, sign receipt, verify with mismatched key
failure_modes.root_cause: verifying.key was replaced without re-signing receipts, or signing.key was replaced
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — old receipts cannot be verified

failure_modes.mode: Key loss — signing.key is deleted or corrupted
failure_modes.symptom: ggen sync fails with 'Could not load signing key'
failure_modes.detection: ls -la .ggen/keys/signing.key; file not found or corrupted
failure_modes.root_cause: File was deleted, disk corruption, or permission issue
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — cannot sign new receipts until key is recovered or regenerated

failure_modes.mode: Fail-open — missing key does not fail hard
failure_modes.symptom: ggen sync continues without signing receipt when key is missing
failure_modes.detection: rm .ggen/keys/signing.key; ggen sync; check if receipt is unsigned
failure_modes.root_cause: emit_receipt() has a fallback that writes unsigned receipt on key load failure
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — unsigned receipt is silently accepted

recovery: If private key is exposed: Revoke old key, generate new keypair with ggen init --force-keys, re-sign all future receipts with new key
If key is lost: Regenerate with ggen init --force-keys (old receipts cannot be re-signed but remain in chain)
If key is corrupted: Restore from backup or regenerate if no backup exists
If key pair is mismatched: Identify which key is correct (signing or verifying) and restore or regenerate the other

sabotage_tests.required_outcome: Exit 1; error message: 'Private key file has insecure permissions' OR Warning issued before signing
Exit 0 and regenerate new keypair, OR exit 1 if regeneration disabled

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Replace verifying key with a different one
sabotage_tests.command: ggen init --output /tmp/other_keys --force-keys; cp /tmp/other_keys/verifying.key .ggen/keys/verifying.key; ggen receipt verify .ggen/receipts/latest.json
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: is_valid: false (signature verification fails with wrong key)
Exit 1; error message: 'Invalid hex in signing key' OR 'Failed to load signing key'

security_dimensions.name: Audit Trail
security_dimensions.short_description: All .ggen/receipts/*.json files are immutable proof of operations performed
security_dimensions.what_it_is: The receipts directory (.ggen/receipts/) serves as an immutable audit log of all ggen sync operations. Each receipt is a timestamped, cryptographically signed proof of what happened during that operation. The receipts cannot be deleted or modified without breaking their signatures. This audit trail is the evidence layer that enables retrospective verification of everything the system has done.

key_invariants: Each receipt is written to a uniquely named file (.ggen/receipts/sync-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.json)
Receipts are never overwritten; new receipts are always appended
.ggen/receipts/latest.json points to the most recent receipt (symlink or copy)
All receipts in the directory form a single cryptographic chain
file_locations: .ggen/receipts/latest.json — pointer to most recent receipt (updated each sync)
key_functions: emit_receipt() writes timestamped receipt file and updates latest.json
ReceiptChain::from_files() loads all receipts from directory and validates chain

validation_command.description: List all receipts in chronological order and verify they form a valid chain
validation_command.example: for f in $(ls -1t .ggen/receipts/sync-*.json); do echo $f: $(jq '.timestamp' $f); done
validation_command.expected_output: Receipts listed in reverse chronological order (newest first), all timestamps unique and increasing

audit_trail_validation.procedure: Load all receipts, verify each signature, validate previous_receipt_hash chain
audit_trail_validation.expected: All signatures valid, all chain links valid, all timestamps unique and monotonic
audit_trail_validation.if_invalid: Broken chain or forged receipt detected

audit_trail_invariants.requirement: Receipts are never modified after creation; only new receipts are added
audit_trail_invariants.enforcement: Receipt files are written once and never modified by ggen; signature covers entire receipt
audit_trail_invariants.failure: Modifying a receipt breaks its signature

audit_trail_invariants.requirement: Each receipt has a unique timestamp (no two receipts with identical timestamp)
audit_trail_invariants.enforcement: chrono::Utc::now() is called at receipt creation time, granular to nanoseconds
audit_trail_invariants.failure: Duplicate timestamp implies duplicate or forged receipt

audit_trail_invariants.requirement: Receipts are ordered chronologically; each receipt's timestamp > prior receipt's timestamp
audit_trail_invariants.enforcement: Files are named with YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS prefix, and previous_receipt_hash enforces chain ordering
audit_trail_invariants.failure: Out-of-order receipts suggest tampering or clock skew

audit_trail_invariants.requirement: No gaps in the receipt chain; no receipts are missing or deleted
audit_trail_invariants.enforcement: ReceiptChain::verify() checks that each previous_receipt_hash matches a known receipt
audit_trail_invariants.failure: Orphaned receipt or deleted receipt breaks the chain

audit_trail_invariants.requirement: All historical receipts (.ggen/receipts/sync-*.json) remain in the directory
audit_trail_invariants.enforcement: Receipts are append-only; ggen never deletes receipts
audit_trail_invariants.failure: Missing receipt file indicates tampering or data loss

failure_modes.mode: Decorative completion — receipt not persisted
failure_modes.symptom: ggen sync exits 0 but .ggen/receipts/sync-*.json is not created
failure_modes.detection: ls -la .ggen/receipts/ before and after sync; compare file counts
failure_modes.root_cause: emit_receipt() was not called or failed silently
failure_modes.impact: CRITICAL — no audit trail of what ran

failure_modes.mode: Legacy path contamination — old receipts still exist and chain is broken
failure_modes.symptom: Old .ggen/receipts/sync-old.json exists but is not linked in the chain
failure_modes.detection: Check if all old receipts have valid previous_receipt_hash links
failure_modes.root_cause: Old receipt format was not updated to include chaining
failure_modes.impact: MEDIUM — audit trail has gaps

failure_modes.mode: Fail-open — deleted receipt is not detected
failure_modes.symptom: Missing receipt file does not cause chain validation to fail
failure_modes.detection: Delete a receipt file and run ReceiptChain::verify()
failure_modes.root_cause: verify() does not check that all expected receipts exist
failure_modes.impact: HIGH — tampering (deletion) is not detected

failure_modes.mode: Contract drift — timestamps are not unique
failure_modes.symptom: Two receipts have identical timestamps or reversed timestamps
failure_modes.root_cause: Clock went backward, or timestamps were hardcoded
failure_modes.impact: MEDIUM — cannot determine ordering

recovery: If receipt is missing: Check filesystem backups or version control history (receipts should not be in git, but may be in backup)
If receipts are out of order: Check filesystem timestamps and previous_receipt_hash links; may need to re-emit receipts
If chain is broken: Identify the break point and restore missing receipt from backup or start a new chain
If audit trail is corrupted: Do not continue; investigate how corruption occurred before proceeding

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Delete a receipt file from the middle of the chain
sabotage_tests.command: ls .ggen/receipts/sync-*.json | sort | sed -n '2p' | xargs rm && ggen receipt verify <later-receipt>
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: Chain validation fails; error message: 'Cannot find receipt for hash [hash]'

sabotage_tests.command: jq '.timestamp = "2000-01-01T00:00:00Z"' .ggen/receipts/sync-*.json | head -1 | jq '.timestamp' > /tmp/old.json; ggen receipt verify <that-receipt>
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: Signature verification fails; is_valid: false

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Load all receipts and verify entire chain integrity
sabotage_tests.command: Test code: load all .ggen/receipts/sync-*.json, verify all signatures, validate all chain links
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: All signatures valid AND all previous_receipt_hash links valid

sabotage_tests.sabotage: Verify that receipt timestamps are monotonically increasing
sabotage_tests.required_outcome: No unsorted output; all timestamps in increasing order

integration_validation.description: All 8 dimensions must be validated together in end-to-end tests

comprehensive_test_scenarios.name: Full sync with complete security validation
steps: 1. Run ggen sync with clean state
2. Verify receipt was generated and signed (dimension 1, 2)
5. Verify receipt is chained to prior (or genesis) (dimension 5)
6. Attempt to tamper with receipt and verify detection (dimension 6)
7. Verify keys are in place with correct permissions (dimension 7)
8. Verify audit trail contains the new receipt (dimension 8)

comprehensive_test_scenarios.name: Tampering in each dimension triggers failure
steps: Run scenario: Modify receipt signature → ggen receipt verify fails
Run scenario: Modify lockfile digest → ggen sync --locked fails
Run scenario: Delete receipt → audit trail validation fails
Run scenario: Corrupt key file → ggen sync fails
Verify ALL scenarios fail hard (not fail-open)

before_claiming_security_complete: [ ] Dimension 1: Receipt is generated with .ggen/receipts/latest.json containing all required fields
[ ] Dimension 2: Receipt signature is verified with Ed25519 public key; tampering breaks signature
[ ] Dimension 3: Lockfile has SHA-256 digest for every package; digests are re-verified at sync --locked
[ ] Dimension 4: input_hashes and output_hashes are non-empty and match actual pipeline inputs/outputs
[ ] Dimension 5: Receipt chain is unbroken; each receipt links to previous via previous_receipt_hash
[ ] Dimension 6: All tampering scenarios (5+ per dimension) are caught and cause hard failures
[ ] Dimension 7: Private key has 0600 permissions and is .gitignored; public key is verified
[ ] Dimension 8: Audit trail contains all historical receipts; no gaps in chain
[ ] All 8 dimensions pass sabotage tests (5+ tests per dimension)
[ ] Integration test validates all 8 dimensions together (e2e test passes)
[ ] No fail-open paths: any security violation causes hard failure (exit 1)
[ ] No legacy paths: old receipt/key formats are not accepted unless explicitly handled
